replies.author: @BonRoiPress
replies.text: Hi, would you consider covering the recent, rapid developments in Jamaica? Jamaica has been dubbed the greatest economic turn around in modern history (strong national debt reduction), murder rate has been slashed nearly in half in one year, and many recent and fast development projects like new, highways, parks, many new roads, so on. Still more to improve, but the recent changes are very promising.

comments.author: @RemoteEarthJourneys
comments.text: *Guyana might be the most fascinating country nobody talks about—an oil boom, Amazon wildlife, indigenous cultures, and still so many unanswered questions. Which part surprised you the most? 🌎*

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K
replies.text: Beautifully put — Guyana really is one of the world's best-kept secrets. What surprised us most was Kaieteur Falls: a single drop several times taller than Niagara, hidden deep in the rainforest, and almost nobody outside Guyana has heard of it. Thanks for watching!

replies.author: @matthewhenry-v6e
replies.text:
IN FACT GUYANA WAS THE MAIN BAUXITE PRODUCER FOR THE WORL WAR 2 AIRPLANE PRODUCTION...
During World War II, roughly two-thirds of all Allied aircraft were manufactured using aluminum derived from bauxite mined in Guyana (then British Guiana). Because aluminum is the critical metal for aircraft frames, this ore was a highly strategic, vital resource for the Allied war effort

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K

replies.author: @matthewhenry-v6e
replies.text: @PlanetVista4K "GUYANA" DID NOT "PRODUCE" ANYTHING IN 1450. .....'IN 1492 COLUMBUS SAILED THE OCEAN BLUE"

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K
replies.text: Guyana tidak menghasilkan beras dan gula pada tahun 1450, dan Christopher Columbus tidak pernah mendarat di sana.

replies.author: @matthewhenry-v6e
replies.text: NO! NO! NO!...Christopher Columbus never actually set foot on the soil of what is now Guyana. During his third voyage in 1498, his fleet sailed along the northern coast of South America, and he sighted the Guyanese coastline, but he never made landfall.....REMEMBER THIS "IN 1492 COLUMBUS SAILED THE OCEAN BLUE".....NOT 1450..

comments.text: Why are there no security railings along the falls?

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K
replies.text: That's a great observation! Safety regulations can vary quite a bit by location, and sometimes natural attractions maintain a more untouched feel.

comments.author: @Rod-n2j1z
comments.text: Everybody own a home there in the USA that history thank the tax man and banker

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K
replies.text: Interesting perspective! Homeownership in the USA definitely has a complex history tied to taxes and banking.

replies.author: @LawyersCouncil-h7w
replies.text: ​@PlanetVista4K❤❤❤❤❤🎉🎉

comments.author: @VittorioKelly
comments.text: If the video also explored environmental safeguards and local jobs, it would balance the booming narrative

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K
replies.text: That’s a great point, balancing the economic growth with those vital perspectives is definitely something to consider for future storytelling. ❤️

comments.author: @KübraGarcia
comments.text: Guyana seems like a hidden gem for nature lovers; this kind of travel content deserves more spotlight

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K
replies.text: It truly is an incredible hidden gem, thank you for the kind support! ❤️

comments.author: @SmithBritt-p8i
comments.text: The film gives a big-picture view, but I would like more on day-to-day challenges people face as growth accelerates

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K
replies.text: I appreciate your thoughtful suggestion, and I’ll definitely keep that in mind for future documentaries about the realities of daily life! ❤️

comments.author: @가은Thompson
comments.text: Interesting mix of pristine nature and a fast-growing oil boom; hope the benefits trickle down to local communities without getting bulldozed 🤔

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K
replies.text: That is such an important perspective to consider as the country continues its rapid development! 😊

comments.author: @IidaEdwards
comments.text: I love how the documentary shows everyday life in remote villages; it feels authentic and warm

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K
replies.text: Thank you for noticing the beauty of village life, it was truly special to experience firsthand. ❤️

comments.author: @AnastasiaRivera-o6k
comments.text: The 4K visuals are stunning, Guyana looks absolutely breathtaking—would love to visit those rainforests and Kaieteur Falls someday 😮

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K
replies.text: I’m so glad you enjoyed the visuals, the natural beauty of Guyana is truly something special! 😊

comments.author: @RelaxHistoryAI
comments.text: This video shows a very different Guyana from what I knew

replies.author: @nvkotian7539
replies.text: I fully agree with you my dear! From Mumbai

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K
replies.text: It’s truly a country with so much evolving, thank you for watching and sharing your thoughts! ❤️

comments.author: @XPlanet-TV
comments.text: I had only heard of Guyana from looking at maps, but this video showed me how quickly the country is changing. Thanks for such a beautiful and informative documentary!

replies.author: @bethewalt7385
replies.text: That's sad, I knew of it in 5th grade 50 year's ago, kiddo's in the last 30 years are woefully uneducated

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K
replies.text: I'm so glad you enjoyed learning about the incredible transformation happening in Guyana! 😊

comments.author: @trongnghia31-10
comments.text: 5:38 This is the first time I've heard about this country.

replies.author: @leannboser3128
replies.text: It’s kind of funny that I have mentioned Guyana to numerous locals here in Costa Rica and they haven’t heard of it either. To me that is like being from California and not knowing New Jersey exists.

replies.author: @trueunrealglobe
replies.text: Watching this made me realize that travel isn't just about destinations. It's about understanding the people, history, and culture behind every place.

replies.author: @RelaxHistoryAI
replies.text: me too
